builds.name: graphical Warp app validation launch build
builds.note: Additional existing skip_firebase_anonymous_user feature was used only to bypass login/onboarding in the isolated graphical validation profile and reach a visible terminal workspace.

setup_evidence.visible_state: External xterm staggered left; target Warp window staggered right with a single visible ~ tab and active terminal pane containing validation setup output.
setup_evidence.permission_evidence: logs/final_discovery_inst_a39b3e2f31854cf9a3f635d1bd2ba77d.json shows outside_warp_control_enabled true and endpoint/credential broker present.

commands.context: outside-warp invocation from external xterm, targeting one visible graphical Warp OSS window in Xvfb/Openbox

permission_state.other_permission_categories: not enabled / not required for this metadata-read command family
permission_state.how_enabled: preseeded private local user_preferences.json for isolated WARP_DATA_PROFILE; final_discovery log shows outside_warp_control_enabled true

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: List windows while one Warp window is visible; compare JSON window count/title/active flag to the visible Warp window.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: No mutation; the visible Warp window/tab/pane/session should remain available and align with returned metadata, or remain unchanged for denial/parser cases.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: The screenshot shows one visible Warp window titled with the active tab/workspace (~), and JSON lists one active window with title ~ and window_id 0.
visual_inspection.ambiguities: For metadata reads, opaque IDs are not directly visible in Warp UI; visual validation compared visible counts, active state, tab title, pane/session presence, and unchanged target state against JSON.

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Keep the valid Warp target visible, then request a bogus window id; verify structured stale/missing target error and no visible UI change.
commands.expected_result: ok=false JSON error with stale_target or missing_target for missing window id

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: No mutation; the visible Warp window/tab/pane/session should remain available and align with returned metadata, or remain unchanged for denial/parser cases.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: The screenshot shows Warp still unchanged with the same visible target; CLI JSON error is ok=false code stale_target, message cannot resolve requested window id.
visual_inspection.ambiguities: For metadata reads, opaque IDs are not directly visible in Warp UI; visual validation compared visible counts, active state, tab title, pane/session presence, and unchanged target state against JSON.

commands.proof_setup_answer: Keep one visible tab, then request tab index 999; verify structured missing/stale selector error and no visible UI change.

visual_inspection.ambiguities: For metadata reads, opaque IDs are not directly visible in Warp UI; visual validation compared visible counts, active state, tab title, pane/session presence, and unchanged target state against JSON.

commands.proof_setup_question: What is the best way to show the impact of this CLI command?
commands.proof_setup_answer: Invoke conflicting selector flags while the visible target remains available; verify parser conflict error before dispatch.
commands.expected_result: CLI conflict error for mutually exclusive --window and --window-index selectors
commands.actual_result:
error: the argument '--window <WINDOW>' cannot be used with '--window-index <WINDOW_INDEX>'

Usage: warpctrl window inspect --window <WINDOW>

For more information, try '--help'.

visual_inspection.expected_visual_effect: No mutation; the visible Warp window/tab/pane/session should remain available and align with returned metadata, or remain unchanged for denial/parser cases.
visual_inspection.observed_visual_effect: The screenshot shows the visible Warp target unchanged; CLI parser rejects --window with --window-index and exits 2.
visual_inspection.ambiguities: For metadata reads, opaque IDs are not directly visible in Warp UI; visual validation compared visible counts, active state, tab title, pane/session presence, and unchanged target state against JSON.
